growth_records.strain: S. ovata (DSM standard strain, not further specified)
growth_records.medium: mineral salts (minimal) medium with NaHCO3 as sole carbon source

up.method: reported (H2 supply rate 2.40 ± 0.19 mmol d-1, e- supply rate 4.80 ± 0.37 mmol d-1, under flowing CO2/N2 electroautotrophic condition)

sec.method: reported, based on temporal trajectory of acetate accumulation

growth_records.cite: Xie et al. Unexpected metabolic rewiring of CO2 fixation in H2-mediated materials–biology hybrids. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2308373120. [PMC10589654]
growth_records.snippet: the H2 supply rate is 2.40 ± 0.19 millimole per day (mmole d-1)... the acetate is the almost exclusive soluble organic end product and accumulation rate of acetate was determined as 0.67 ± 0.08 mmole d-1... the acetate p
growth_records.notes: Rates reported as whole-culture accumulation/supply rates (mmol per day) in a bioelectrochemical/gas-fed batch system, not normalized per gDW biomass (paper does not report biomass concentration alongside these rates), so uptake/secretion values are recorded in mmol/day rather than mmol/gDW/h and cannot be converted to specific rates without a reported biomass value; therefore confidence is medium and units intentionally left as reported rather than mmol/gDW/h. Culture grown anaerobically at 30°C in mineral salts medium with NaHCO3 as sole C source, cysteine 0.2 mM as reducing agent, per Methods 'Chemoautotrophic Culturing of S. ovata'. No growth rate (mu) or doubling time reported in text.

up.method: autotrophic C source from CO2/H2 headspace; consumption not quantified per biomass in text

sec.method: 1H-qNMR of culture medium; delta[acetate]/time = 25 mM over first 24 h from ~0 autotrophic baseline

growth_records.cite: Cestellos-Blanco S et al. Photosynthetic biohybrid coculture for tandem and tunable CO2 and N2 fixation. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (PNAS) (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2122364119. [PMC9245687]
growth_records.snippet: We inoculated S. ovata in autotrophic medium (pH 6.8, 35 °C) at three sequential starting optical densities (OD): I (starting OD545 of 0.15) ... Over the first 24 h, III reached an acetate concentration of 34 mM, while I and II reached 25 a
growth_records.notes: Volumetric acetate secretion rate DERIVED: 25 mM acetate reached over first 24 h (monoculture I) from autotrophic H2/CO2, baseline ~0; rate = 25 mM / 24 h = 1.04 mM/h. Cannot normalize to gDW: biomass tracked only as OD545 (grew by ~0.2), no gDW conversion given. mu/doubling not computable (OD over multiple days, no clean exponential slope in text). Medium composition in SI Appendix only, not main text. Methods also state starting pH 7.2 for autotrophic revival; growth experiments state pH 6.8, 35 C.
